_meta.description: Complete coaching command library for FORMA / ExerVision — with variants, escalation, and external cues
categories: visibility — body parts not visible to camera
correction — form errors during the movement (with variants + external cues)
phase_cue  — positive guidance for current movement phase
fix_ack    — acknowledging when user corrects an issue
compensation — root cause chains linking related issues

escalation_tiers.urgent: 4+ occurrences — strong warning, may suggest stopping

cue_types.external: Focus on environment/effect (e.g., 'push the floor away') — research shows 2x more effective (Wulf 2013)

streak.3: 3 in a row — keep it going
streak.5: 5 solid reps — you're in a groove

set_complete: Done — nice work on that set

depth: That's better — depth is there now
Good fix — much deeper that rep
There it is — that's the depth you want
alignment: You straightened it out — maintain that
stability: Much more controlled — keep it steady
Tighter — that's the control you need
safety: Better angle — joints will thank you
You corrected it — much safer now
generic: You fixed it — keep that up

breathing.general: Keep breathing — don't hold your breath
breathing.brace: Big breath in, brace your core
breathing.reminder: Remember to breathe through the movement
breathing.rhythm: Find a breathing rhythm — in on the easy part, out on the hard part

tempo.pause_bottom: Try pausing at the bottom for a count
tempo.negative: Slow the lowering phase — that's where strength is built

mild: Form starting to drift — stay focused
Getting a bit sloppy — tighten up
moderate: Losing form — consider ending the set
Form is breaking down — one or two more max
Quality is dropping — better to stop clean
stop: Form has broken down — rack it, rest, reset
Stop — form is gone, rest before next set
End the set — pushing through bad form builds bad habits

safety.pain: If you feel sharp pain, stop immediately
safety.reduce_weight: Consider reducing weight to maintain form
safety.joint_stress: Joint under excessive stress — reduce range or weight
safety.stop_set: End the set — protect yourself for the next one

duration.30: 30 seconds — halfway to a good hold

warning: Slight imbalance between left and right
One side is working harder — even it out
bad: Significant left/right imbalance — {side} side weaker ({diff}° diff)
Big difference between sides — {side} is lagging ({diff}°)

visibility.general: Step into frame — full body must be visible
visibility.too_close: Step back — camera is too close

setup.text: Stand facing the camera — knees must be visible
Stand up straight to begin

visibility.text: Can't see knees and hips — adjust your camera

variants: Go deeper — thighs should reach parallel
A bit more depth — aim for parallel
Keep dropping — not deep enough yet
depth_shallow.external_cue: Push the floor away as you come up from deeper

escalation.gentle: Try to get a little deeper each rep
escalation.firm: You need to squat deeper — parallel is the target
escalation.urgent: Depth hasn't improved — focus only on this

variants: Squat deeper — thighs need to reach parallel
Way too shallow — drop down further
That's a quarter squat — go deeper
depth_very_shallow.external_cue: Sit back into an invisible chair behind you

escalation.gentle: Try sitting back more — imagine a chair behind you
escalation.firm: Squat deeper — thighs need to reach parallel
escalation.urgent: Depth is not there — try box squats to learn the range

variants: Drop hips lower — aim for hip crease below knee
Hips need to go lower — crease below knee
Sit deeper into the squat
hip_shallow.external_cue: Imagine sitting down onto a low box

variants: Keep chest up — reduce forward lean
Chest is dropping — stay more upright
Torso tilting forward — chest proud
torso_lean_mild.external_cue: Point your chest at the wall in front of you

escalation.gentle: Try to keep your chest a bit more upright
escalation.firm: Keep chest up — you're leaning too far forward
escalation.urgent: Forward lean is persistent — try front squats to build the pattern

variants: Excessive forward lean — keep torso upright
You're folding over — chest up, chest up
Way too much lean — this puts strain on your back
torso_lean_severe.external_cue: Drive your back into an imaginary wall behind you

variants: Slight knee cave — focus on pushing knees outward
Knees drifting in a bit — track them over your toes
Watch your knees — they're collapsing inward slightly
knee_valgus_mild.external_cue: Spread the floor apart with your feet

escalation.gentle: Try to keep your knees tracking over your toes
escalation.firm: Knees are caving — push them outward
escalation.urgent: Knee valgus is a real injury risk — reduce weight or widen stance

variants: Knees caving in — injury risk, push knees out over toes
Serious knee collapse — this can hurt your ACL
Knees are way in — stop and reset your stance
knee_valgus_severe.external_cue: Screw your feet into the floor — turn them outward

variants: Limited ankle mobility — try a wider stance or heel elevation
Ankles are tight — put small plates under your heels
Ankle restriction limiting your depth — work on calf stretches
Ankle tight — consider wider stance for better depth
Limited ankle range — try squat shoes or heel wedges
Good morning pattern — drive chest up with the hips
Hips rising without your chest — lead with the chest
You're turning it into a good morning — chest and hips together
good_morning.external_cue: Push your back into the bar as you stand up

variants: Heels lifting — keep weight on heels
Stay flat-footed — heels are coming up
Weight shifting to toes — press through your heels
heel_rise.external_cue: Grip the floor with your toes and press heels down

variants: Butt wink — pelvis tucking under at bottom
Lower back rounding at the bottom — pelvis is tucking
Posterior pelvic tilt at depth — stop just above this point
Slight butt wink — try stopping just above this depth
Minor pelvic tuck at the bottom — not a problem yet

ankle_to_lean.text: Your forward lean might be caused by tight ankles — try heel elevation

ankle_to_heel.text: Heels lifting because of tight ankles — work on calf mobility

lean_to_good_morning.text: The good morning pattern starts with the forward lean — fix that first

variants: Good — begin rep
Ready — go down
Set — drop it
Good — keep lowering
Controlled descent
Nice and steady
Good depth! Drive back up
That's parallel — push up
Solid depth — explode up
Push! Drive through your heels
Up! Chest first
Stand it up — strong finish

breathing.descend: Big breath in — brace before you descend
breathing.ascend: Breathe out as you drive up
breathing.reminder: Brace your core — big breath at the top of each rep

tempo.too_fast: Slow the descent — control the weight down

regression.depth: Try box squats — sit back to a box at parallel to learn the depth
regression.balance: Goblet squats build control — try those to reinforce the pattern
regression.mobility: Work on ankle and hip mobility before adding weight

setup.text: Stand with feet hip-width apart — both legs must be visible
Side view works best for lunges

visibility.text: Both legs must be fully visible — hips to feet

variants: Go deeper — front thigh should be parallel to floor
Drop lower — front knee should hit 90 degrees
More depth on the front leg
front_knee_shallow.external_cue: Lower your back knee toward the floor

escalation.gentle: Try to get a little deeper each rep
escalation.firm: You need more depth — front thigh parallel to floor
escalation.urgent: Shallow lunges aren't training the full range — commit to depth

variants: Drop back knee closer to floor
Back knee should almost touch the ground
Lower the back knee — hover it just above the floor
back_knee_high.external_cue: Touch your back knee to a towel on the floor

variants: Keep torso upright — don't lean forward
Chest is dropping — stay tall
Stand taller through the lunge
torso_lean_mild.external_cue: Imagine a string pulling the top of your head to the ceiling

variants: Excessive forward lean — chest up!
You're folding forward — stay upright
Way too much lean — this shifts load off the legs
torso_lean_severe.external_cue: Keep your eyes on the horizon line — don't look down

variants: Hips tilting to one side — engage glutes and core
Pelvis is uneven — squeeze your glutes to level out
Hip drop detected — focus on glute activation
pelvic_tilt_moderate.external_cue: Keep your belt line level — imagine balancing a glass of water on your hips

variants: Slight hip drop — keep pelvis level
Minor Trendelenburg — strengthen that glute med
Front knee tracking too far forward — keep shin vertical
Knee is over your toes — take a bigger step
Step further out — knee shouldn't pass your toes
front_knee_past_toe.external_cue: Take a longer step so your shin stays vertical

variants: Widen your stance — feet too close together for balance
Step wider — you're on a tightrope
More hip-width between your feet for stability

weak_glute_to_lean.text: Your forward lean may be compensating for weak glutes — focus on hip stability first

variants: Good — step forward
Ready — step out
Set — go
Good — keep lowering
Controlled descent
Dropping nicely
Good depth! Drive back up
Solid lunge — push back
Deep enough — explode up
Push through the front heel
Drive up! Front heel
Strong push — back to start

breathing.descend: Breathe in as you step and lower
breathing.ascend: Breathe out as you push back up

tempo.too_fast: Slow the step — control the descent

regression.balance: Try stationary lunges first — no stepping, just up and down
regression.depth: Use a pad under your back knee as a depth target

setup.text: Stand sideways to the camera — hips must be visible
Stand tall with soft knees to begin

visibility.text: Can't see hips and shoulders — side view works best

variants: Hinge a bit deeper — push hips back
More hip hinge — push your butt back further
Not deep enough — reach further toward the floor

variants: Push your hips back and hinge forward

This needs to be a hip movement — hinge, don't squat

variants: Stand up fully at the top
Finish the rep — full lockout
Hips forward at the top — stand tall
not_locked_out.external_cue: Push your hips into the bar at the top

variants: Try to keep legs a bit straighter
Less knee bend — this is a hip hinge, not a squat
Knees are bending too much — push hips back instead
knee_too_bent_mild.external_cue: Let the bar pull you forward from your hips, not down through your knees

variants: Too much knee bend — push hips back instead
You're squatting the deadlift — hinge at the hips
Knees way too bent — this should feel like a hip hinge
knee_too_bent_severe.external_cue: Imagine you're doing a bow — hips back, legs mostly straight

variants: Keep chest up — back starting to round
Upper back rounding — pull your shoulders back
Spine is flexing — stay neutral

escalation.gentle: Focus on keeping your chest proud
escalation.firm: Back is rounding — this is where injuries happen
escalation.urgent: Back rounding hasn't improved — reduce the weight

variants: Back rounding — straighten up, chest proud

back_rounding_severe.external_cue: Stick your chest out — proud posture

variants: Lead with your chest — hips shooting up
Hips are rising first — stay with it
Chest is falling behind — drive both up together

variants: Keep the bar close to your body — it's drifting forward
Bar is swinging away — drag it up your legs
Keep the weight close — it should graze your shins

setup.text: Position camera to the side — both arms must be visible

Full extension at the top — lock it

variants: Elbows too wide — impingement risk, tuck arms

You lost your arch — retract your scapulae

Touch and go — don't slam it off your chest

Good — lower with control
Slow negative — good
Controlled descent
Full contraction! Squeeze at the top
Squeeze the bicep — hold it
Top of the curl — squeeze
Good — lower slowly
Slow it down on the way down
Control the descent

breathing.descend: Breathe in as you lower the weight
breathing.ascend: Breathe out as you curl up

tempo.too_fast: Slow the negative — the eccentric builds muscle
tempo.momentum: Don't use momentum — isolate the bicep

regression.weight: Drop the weight — strict form with light weight beats cheating with heavy
regression.technique: Try concentration curls (seated) to learn the isolation

setup.text: Position camera to see arms — elbows must be visible
Extend arms fully to start — press up on the bench

visibility.text: Arms and shoulders must be visible

variants: Going too deep — stop at 85-90° to protect shoulders
Too much depth — this puts your shoulder capsule at risk
Ease up on the depth — 90 degrees is plenty
too_deep.external_cue: Stop when your upper arm is parallel to the floor — no further

variants: Go a bit deeper — bend elbows closer to 90°
More range — you're barely bending
Deeper — you need to feel a stretch at the bottom
too_shallow.external_cue: Lower until your upper arm is parallel to the ground

escalation.gentle: Try getting a bit more depth on each rep
escalation.firm: You need more depth — aim for 90 degree bend
escalation.urgent: Shallow dips aren't training the muscle — commit to the range

variants: Extend arms fully at top
Lock out — straighten your arms completely
Finish the rep — full extension
lockout_incomplete.external_cue: Push yourself away from the bench — arms straight

variants: Watch shoulder extension — reducing depth protects the joint
Shoulders are being stretched too far — ease up on depth
Shoulder strain building — don't go so deep
Shoulder impingement risk — come up, reduce depth
Shoulders are compressed — dangerous position, come up
Impingement territory — reduce depth immediately
Excessive forward lean — reduces triceps emphasis
Leaning forward shifts load to chest — stay upright for triceps
More upright — you're turning this into a chest exercise
forward_lean.external_cue: Keep your back close to the bench — stay vertical

variants: Elbows flaring — keep them tucked back
Elbows going out — point them straight behind you
Tuck your elbows — they're drifting sideways
elbow_flare_mild.external_cue: Point your elbows at the wall behind you, not the walls beside you

variants: Elbows flaring excessively — tuck closer to body
Elbows way out — this stresses the shoulder joint
Major elbow flare — bring them in tight
Shoulders sinking below elbows — control depth
Shoulders dropped too low — don't let them pass your elbows
Shoulder position is too low — come up a bit
Keep hips close to the bench
Stay close to the bench — don't drift forward
Hips should be right next to the bench
Hips too far from bench — stay close
Scoot back — you're too far from the bench
Close the gap between your hips and the bench
Good lockout — begin descent
Arms locked — go down
Good — lower with control
Good — lower with control
Controlled descent
Steady down
Good depth! Press up
90 degrees — push!
Full range — drive up
Push! Lock out at the top
Drive — arms straight
Up! Full extension

breathing.ascend: Breathe out as you press up

tempo.too_fast: Control the descent — don't just drop into it
tempo.momentum: Don't bounce at the bottom — controlled reps

regression.depth: If shoulders hurt, reduce the depth — you don't need full 90 degrees
regression.strength: Feet further from the bench makes it easier — adjust leg position
